summary.intent: Fix five Codex-identified review issues in PR #710 (impl-285 branch of fogodev/ars-ui), push the fixes, resolve the review threads, and trigger a fresh Codex review pass.
summary.outcome: All five original findings were fixed in commit dea1abb3 and pushed; threads were resolved and a fresh Codex pass was triggered. Codex returned three new findings (two GridList state-machine edge cases, one stale typeahead spec doc), which were also fixed, tested, and committed as a follow-up.

repo: The repo uses a `snapshot-reviewed` GitHub label to track PRs with snapshot changes; the label must be applied manually after each snapshot-affecting push.
Codex review is triggered by posting `@codex review` as a PR comment and tracked via GitHub reactions (eyes = in progress) and unresolved review threads.
The typeahead API uses `Duration` and `TYPEAHEAD_TIMEOUT` constants — not raw millisecond integers; old `TYPEAHEAD_TIMEOUT_MS` and integer timestamp examples are stale.
Disabled GridList composite roots must use `tabindex=-1`; disabled link cells must omit `href` while preserving disabled-state attributes.
Space key dispatch in GridList must honor `selection_behavior` and emit `Select` for Replace mode.
Non-composite list cells must omit cell-level `aria-selected`; the row/listitem carries selection state.
Invalid endpoint keys in the GridList state machine must be rejected rather than silently converted to 'no target'.
The project uses `cargo xtask ci clippy`, `cargo xtask spec validate`, `cargo xtask spec compile-snippets`, and `cargo xcov` as CI gates before committing.
Coverage gate checks branch coverage thresholds per crate; `ars-components` has an enforced branch coverage minimum.

code.finding: State-machine endpoint key validation: invalid keys were previously converted to 'no target' instead of being rejected; guards were added to reject them explicitly, with regression tests.
Public API is `process_char` accepting `Duration` and disabled-key/disabled-behavior arguments — not the old integer-timestamp form. Callers and docs must use `TYPEAHEAD_TIMEOUT` constant.
Typeahead examples were stale: showed millisecond integers and old argument shapes. Updated to use `Duration` and current API signature.
Integration test spec examples for typeahead also used old millisecond timestamp style; corrected to match current API.

workflow: GitHub GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutation requires thread node IDs (not database IDs); these must be fetched separately before resolving.
Polling Codex review state requires checking both PR reactions (eyes reaction = in progress) and unresolved threads filtered by author login matching 'codex'.
A two-minute polling interval was sufficient for Codex to complete a review pass; CI jobs took longer.
The correct commit flow is: stage specific files, run all local gates (fmt, clippy, tests, coverage, spec), then commit — not the reverse.
Sandbox network restrictions can block `gh` commands; re-running with approved network escalation resolves the issue.

friction: Sandbox network restrictions blocked the initial `gh pr edit` label command, requiring a re-run with network escalation approval.
Codex review required multiple 2-minute polling intervals (at least 4 cycles, ~10+ minutes) before returning findings.

The stale typeahead spec examples required updating not just the numeric literals but also the full argument shapes to match the current API, making the fix more involved than a simple number replacement.
Staged vs. unstaged file state was inconsistent going into the follow-up commit (spec files were pre-staged from a prior state), requiring careful diff inspection before staging.
open_items: CI coverage and release verification jobs were still pending at the time the follow-up commit was pushed — their final status is not confirmed in the transcript.
The Dioxus feature-flag CI job was still pending when the follow-up commit was staged; its outcome is unknown.
A fresh `@codex review` was not explicitly re-triggered after the follow-up commit — the transcript ends at `git commit`; the new findings may trigger another review cycle.
